codeReview.summary: The observability-audit-trail feature implements an immutable append-only event log with SHA256 cryptographic chaining for EU AI Act Article 12 compliance. The implementation in swell-core/src/audit.rs provides: AuditEntry struct with prev_hash/hash chaining, AuditLog manager for append-only NDJSON storage, verify_chain() for integrity verification, and helper methods for logging tool calls, state transitions, decisions, and session events. All 10 audit-specific tests pass, covering hash chain integrity, tamper detection, and chain verification.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The handoff commitId (404f7f2) points to the events.rs (OpenTelemetry schema) commit, not the audit.rs commit (966a548). While the worker correctly identified and verified the existing audit.rs implementation, the commit reference in the handoff is misleading.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff file: 2026-04-10T05-49-23-588Z__observability-audit-trail__16baf1b9-c563-4515-af07-0a2abb5fc1ce.json - commitId field shows '404f7f2' but git log shows audit.rs was added in '966a548'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The audit log file path uses a hardcoded constant AUDIT_FILE = 'audit/swell-actions.ndjson' which is relative to the working directory. AGENTS.md doesn't document any conventions for file path handling in audit/logging contexts.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: audit.rs:18 - const AUDIT_FILE: &str = "audit/swell-actions.ndjson"; The path is not configurable at runtime beyond the initial AuditLog::new() call.

summary: PASS - The observability-audit-trail feature is correctly implemented. The audit log provides immutable append-only storage with SHA256 cryptographic chaining (prev_hash/hash fields), chain integrity verification via verify_chain(), and tamper detection. The implementation covers all expected behaviors: events appended immutably, previous hash included in each event, chain integrity verifiable, no modification of past events, and tampering detection. All 10 audit-specific tests pass, including tests for hash chain integrity, tamper detection (modified entry, missing entry), and deterministic hash computation.
